nodes.label: Bucca: "Black-White differences in intergenerational mobility in the US: evidence from heterogeneous sibling correlations"
nodes.author: Mauricio Bucca

nodes.label: Concept: "perverse openness" among African Americans in social mobility
nodes.rationale: Classic term from Blau and Duncan (1967) that the paper revisits and resolves: the weak observed association between Black social origins and destinations is shown to stem mainly from higher within-family (sibling) heterogeneity rather than a genuinely different intergenerational elasticity, reconciling it with Bloome and Western's finding of similar IGEs for Blacks and Whites.
